judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently arrive at essentially identical, fully correct root causes and fixes for all three expectations; Report 2 adds a sharper critique of the existing test suite's blind spot, but overall the two are comparable in substance and completeness.

report_1.evidence: Postgres row-level security is bypassed unconditionally for superusers, and also for table owners unless the table has FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY set — which 0001_init.sql never sets. So cedar_owner is exempt from RLS for two independent reasons at once.

report_2.evidence: In Postgres, RLS policies are never applied to superusers, and by default are not applied to a table's owner either (that requires ALTER TABLE ... FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, which 0001_init.sql never adds — and even if it did, superuser status alone bypasses RLS regardless of FORCE).

report_1.evidence: Keep cedar_owner (or an equivalently privileged schema-owning role) reserved exclusively for scripts/migrate.sh / schema migrations — never for serving live app traffic. Concretely, use two separate env vars (e.g. DATABASE_URL for the app, MIGRATIONS_DATABASE_URL for migrate.sh) so the two roles can never be accidentally collapsed into one connection string again

report_2.evidence:
cedar_owner (or a dedicated migration role) should be used only by scripts/migrate.sh / CI migration jobs to create/alter schema — never by the running API.
  - cedar_app should be the only role the FastAPI process ever authenticates as, and it should keep least-privilege grants

report_1.evidence: The cedar_app role is created only by cedarline/db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql, which is explicitly commented "Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup,"... reset-local-db.sh then only calls ./scripts/migrate.sh, which runs the db/migrations/*.sql files as cedar_owner — none of those migrations create cedar_app; only the bootstrap script does, and reset-local-db.sh never invokes it.

report_2.evidence: cedar_app is not created by this process at all: it only ever gets created by manually running db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql (explicitly documented in that file as "One-time bootstrap... Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup,"... reset-local-db.sh ... only re-runs migrate.sh — it never re-invokes create_app_role.sql, and docker-compose.yml has no /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d mount that would auto-run it either.
